body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-14 morning: 'T-typed-memories-to-tags can be s. we just start behaving that way (stop creating new types) and when we go to access one that needs updating, we update it. simple.'

BIOMIMETIC REASON: the brain doesn't have memory types. Same neurons store everything; the procedural/episodic/declarative distinction is about which retrieval circuit fires, not about what the storage substrate is. Types are a retrieval-path label, not a storage fact. A memory that has to know its own type before being used can't participate in cross-circuit integration — exactly what T-goals-as-persistent-relationships (#422) is trying to break down (goals as PRs with abstract second-nodes rather than their own type).

CURRENT TYPES: ROOT, CP, ID, RM, EPISODIC, PROCEDURAL, INTERPRETIVE, EXPERIENTIAL, FACTUAL. They do three jobs:
1. Inertia defaults — already computed from structure (depth + activation + children), could drop type term
2. Retrieval routing — get_by_type for fast filtering; tags+indexes do this with more flexibility
3. Code-path dispatch — brittle, already getting weird post-D260 (payload blurs PROC/EPISODIC boundary)

APPROACH (deliberately NOT a big bang):
1. **Stop creating new types.** Any new type-shaped distinction becomes a tag in metadata, not an enum value. This is the only immediate behavioral change.
2. **Opportunistic conversion.** When touching code that reads/writes a memory_type, convert that path to tag-based at the same time. No sweep. No dedicated sprint. Just: if you're there anyway, fix it.
3. **Tag substrate verification.** Before step 1 is real, verify metadata tags + indexes can carry everything types carry: fast filter by tag (add GIN index on metadata->'tags' if missing), tag-based inertia lookup, tag-based retrieval routing in cortex.
4. **Let the type column become vestigial.** Over time it stops being the source of truth; it's one tag among many. Final removal is far-future HIGH-inertia work, not this ticket.

DO NOT:
- Sweep-refactor all existing memories
- Drop the memory_type column
- Touch models.py beyond adding tag index if needed

EXISTING WORK TO CHECK:
- memory/models.py — MEMORY_TYPES enum, current type→inertia mapping
- memory/cortex.py — get_by_type(), type-filtered queries
- D260 payload — already demonstrates PROC/EPISODIC blur; review whether tag approach would be cleaner there too
- metadata tag usage in existing memories — is there already an informal tag convention?
- GIN index on metadata — #261 added narrative tsvector GIN; check if metadata has similar

DEPENDENCIES: none hard. Aligns with #422 (goals as PRs not a new type) and the whole everything-is-memory DP1 principle.
body.title: Deprecate memory types in favor of tags — opportunistic, not big-bang
body.result: Pattern established: MemoryType enum frozen, new distinctions go in metadata tags. DECISION type removed (last row converted to FACTUAL today). store_note demonstrates the tag pattern (note_category). CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md already document the rule.
